name: WILLAMETTE VALLEY VINEYARDS INC
business: Willamette Valley Vineyards produces and sells premium, super premium, and ultra-premium wines under multiple labels including Willamette Valley Vineyards, Domaine Willamette, and Griffin Creek. The company sells its wine directly to consumers through tasting rooms, wine club memberships, and its e-commerce website, as well as to independent distributors and wine brokers for resale to retail outlets, restaurants, and hotels nationwide.

exposures.sentence: The Company was incorporated in May 1988 to produce and sell premium, super premium and ultra-premium varietals.

Under its Willamette Valley Vineyards label, the Company produces and sells the following types of wine in 750 ml bottles: Pinot Noir, the brand's flagship and its largest selling varietal, $25 to $120 per bottle; Chardonnay, $25 to $85 per bottle; Pinot Gris, $35 per bottle; Pinot Blanc, $40 per bottle; Sauvignon Blanc, $35 per bottle; Gruner Veltliner, $45 per bottle; Rose, $27 to $34 per bottle; Brut, $80 per bottle; Brut Rose, $80 per bottle; and Riesling, $21 per bottle.
The Company sells its wine through its own e-commerce website.

tamClaims.claim: US wine market revenue in 2025 was approximately $74.3 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Silicon Valley Bank's (SVB) 2026 annual report, US wine volume reduced by around 2% to 329 million cases and revenue reduced 1.6% to around $74.3 billion in 2025 from 2024.

tamClaims.claim: Oregon wine grape crop value in 2024 was $329 million.
tamClaims.sentence: Oregon wine grapes produced a 2024 crop with a total value of $329 million, a decrease of 6% from 2023.
